questions.title: How should `verify` enumerate package tests?
questions.context: Current root `package.json:11` runs only `@protostar/intent` and `@protostar/factory-cli` tests. CONCERNS.md flags this — the 4684-line `policy/src/admission-control.test.ts` is not in the gate. Every package already has `test: pnpm run build && node --test dist/*.test.js`.

options.label: `pnpm -r test` (run every package with a test script)
options.description: Simplest, future-proof — new packages auto-join the gate. Trades a small amount of explicitness for zero drift.

options.label: Explicit allowlist of all 11 packages in the script
options.description: Verbose but auditable. Adding a package requires editing `verify` — protects against an unintended package being silently added.

options.label: Tiered: fast (typecheck + intent + factory-cli) and full (`pnpm -r test`)
options.description: Local dev gets fast feedback; CI runs full. Adds two scripts (`verify`, `verify:full`) and slightly more cognitive load.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Should `verify` re-exercise every fixture against the live admission path, or trust package tests?
questions.context: Fixtures live in `examples/intents/` and `examples/planning-results/`. PLAN-A-01 says every candidate plan must flow through `parsePlanningPileResult → admitCandidatePlans → assertAdmittedPlanHandoff`. Package tests already feed many of these fixtures, but coverage is per-package, not end-to-end.

options.label: Add a dedicated `admission-fixtures.test.ts` that loops every fixture end-to-end
options.description: One test owns 'no fixture escapes admission'. Lives in factory-cli or a new `admission-e2e` package. Fastest way to lock the invariant.

options.label: Trust per-package tests + assert fixture file count parity in a meta-test
options.description: A small test asserts every file under `examples/intents/` is referenced by at least one test. Cheaper but indirect.

options.label: Both — meta-test for parity, e2e test for happy-path admission
options.description: Belt and suspenders. Catches both 'fixture added but never tested' and 'fixture tested but not e2e'.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How do we prove no `ConfirmedIntent` is constructed outside `promoteIntentDraft`?
questions.context: INTENT-02 demands `promoteIntentDraft` be the only path. `ConfirmedIntent` lives in `packages/intent/src/confirmed-intent/`. There's already a `confirmed-intent-readonly.contract.ts` and a `public-split-exports.contract.test.ts`. The constructor is currently exported.

options.label: Branded type + private constructor — only `promoteIntentDraft` can mint
options.description: TypeScript-level guarantee. Requires moving the type into a sealed factory; downstream consumers receive the branded `ConfirmedIntent` and cannot reconstruct it. Strongest compile-time guard.

options.label: ESLint rule `no-restricted-imports` blocking direct constructor use
options.description: Lint-time. Easy to add but easy to bypass with `// eslint-disable`. Pairs well with (a).

options.label: Architecture test that greps the codebase for forbidden constructions
options.description: Runtime/test-time grep for `new ConfirmedIntent(` or direct property assignment outside the admission module. Cheap, no compiler changes.

options.label: Combine (a) + a contract test that asserts only `promoteIntentDraft` is exported as a mint path
options.description: Defense in depth. Brand at type level + contract test that locks the public surface.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: How is `assertAdmittedPlanHandoff` enforced as the only handoff path into execution?
questions.context: PLAN-A-01 says every candidate plan flows through `parsePlanningPileResult → admitCandidatePlans → assertAdmittedPlanHandoff`. Execution package today reads `AdmittedPlan` directly. The `candidate-admitted-plan-boundary.contract.ts` exists in planning.

options.label: Branded `AdmittedPlan` type — execution accepts only the brand
options.description: Compile-time. `assertAdmittedPlanHandoff` is the only function that produces the brand. Mirrors (Q-03 a).

options.label: Runtime signature — admission stamps a non-forgeable token, execution verifies
options.description: Overlaps with Phase 2 GOV-06 (signed intent). Risk of duplicating Phase 2 work; consider deferring.

options.label: Architecture test asserting execution's only `AdmittedPlan` import path is `@protostar/planning/admission`
options.description: Cheapest. Catches accidental short-circuits but not malicious ones.

options.label: Custom

sections.title: Fuzzed-bad fixtures (success criterion: refuses to advance)

questions.title: Fuzzing approach for the 'fuzzed-bad intent' and 'fuzzed-bad plan' success criteria
questions.context: Roadmap success criteria: 'A fuzzed-bad intent and a fuzzed-bad plan each produce the correct no-admission artifact and refuse to advance.' Current bad fixtures are hand-curated (`examples/intents/bad-missing-capability.json`, `examples/planning-results/bad-*.json`).

options.label: Property-based fuzzing with `fast-check` — generate intents/plans, assert admission rejects all schema-violating ones
options.description: Adds a dependency. Strong guarantee — covers state space the curated fixtures miss. Slower test runtime.

options.label: Snapshot-mutation generator — programmatically corrupt the good `scaffold` fixture and assert each mutant rejects
options.description: Self-contained, deterministic. Mutations like 'drop required field', 'duplicate task id', 'inject unknown AC' systematically applied. No new dependency.

options.label: Expand the curated bad-fixture set to cover every admission rule, no fuzzing
options.description: Simplest. Each rejection rule has at least one bad fixture. Loses generative coverage — only tests what was thought of.

options.label: Combine (b) deterministic mutator + (c) curated edge cases
options.description: Mutator covers schema rules; curated fixtures cover semantic edge cases (cyclic graph, missing AC, capability expansion).

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Where do bad fixtures live, and how are they distinguished from good ones?
questions.context: Existing pattern: `examples/intents/bad-missing-capability.json` (prefix), `examples/planning-results/bad-*.json` (prefix). No registry of which fixture must reject and why.

options.label: Keep `bad-*` prefix in `examples/`, add a `fixtures.manifest.json` listing each fixture and expected verdict
options.description: Manifest is the single source of truth. Tests read it, loop through, assert verdict matches. New fixture without manifest entry fails.

options.label: Move bad fixtures to `examples/intents/bad/` and `examples/planning-results/bad/` subdirs
options.description: Structural separation. Discovery-by-directory (every file in `bad/` must reject). No manifest needed.

options.label: Generate bad fixtures at test time only — keep `examples/` for human-readable good cases
options.description: If fuzzing is the source (Q-05 a/b), bad fixtures live in test memory only. Cleaner tree, harder to inspect what was tested.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Schema versioning for `clarification-report.json` and `no-plan-admitted` artifacts
questions.context: INTENT-01 requires `clarification-report.json`. PLAN-A-02 requires a 'no-plan-admitted artifact'. Phase 2 will add admission decision artifacts that are 'schema-versioned'. Clarification report schema test exists at `packages/intent/src/clarification-report-schema.test.ts`.

options.label: Embed `schemaVersion: "1.0.0"` in every refusal artifact + ship JSON Schemas under `packages/*/schema/`
options.description: Aligns with Phase 2's schema-versioning requirement. Pays the cost upfront so Phase 2 doesn't have to migrate existing artifacts.

options.label: Brand the type only; defer JSON Schema files to Phase 2
options.description: Less ceremony in Phase 1. Risk: artifacts emitted in Phase 1 fixtures can't be replayed against Phase 2 schemas without migration.

options.label: Embed `schemaVersion` field but no separate JSON Schema yet — schema added in Phase 2
options.description: Middle ground. Field reserves the slot, validators come later.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Where are refusal artifacts written on disk?
questions.context: Successful runs write to `.protostar/runs/{id}/`. Refusals today abort early; no consistent home. CONCERNS.md notes 208 run dirs already accumulated on 2026-04-26.

options.label: Under the same `.protostar/runs/{id}/` with a `terminal-status: rejected` marker file
options.description: One run = one directory regardless of outcome. Operator surface (Phase 9) lists all runs uniformly.

options.label: Separate `.protostar/refusals/{id}/` tree
options.description: Keeps successful runs uncluttered. Two query paths for any 'show me everything' tool.

options.label: Same dir as (a), plus a top-level `.protostar/refusals.jsonl` index for fast scans
options.description: Best of both. Append-only index of all refusals; full evidence in run dir.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Where does the cross-package 'draft → confirmed → downstream observes identical AC' test live?
questions.context: INTENT-03: 'Acceptance criteria normalization is contract-tested end-to-end'. Today, `acceptance-criteria-normalization.contract.ts` is in `packages/intent`. Downstream consumers (planning, execution) have their own AC checks but no shared E2E.

options.label: New `packages/admission-e2e/` test-only package importing from intent + planning + execution
options.description: Owns cross-cutting contracts. Future E2E tests (Phase 2 capability handoff, Phase 3 repo-scope) accumulate here.

options.label: In `apps/factory-cli/src/admission-e2e.test.ts` — CLI is already the integrator
options.description: Reuses an existing test home. Risk: CLI tests grow into a catch-all.

options.label: In `packages/intent` with mock planning/execution consumers
options.description: Self-contained but dishonest — mocks can drift from real downstream parsers.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: What does 'identical AC' mean structurally — by-id, by-content-hash, or by-deep-equal?
questions.context: AC normalization may collapse whitespace, sort fields, etc. Downstream packages must observe the same AC the user confirmed.

options.label: Deep equal on the whole `AcceptanceCriterion[]` array post-normalization
options.description: Strongest. Any divergence (ordering, casing, extra field) is caught.

options.label: Stable content hash embedded in `ConfirmedIntent`, downstream re-hashes and compares
options.description: Cheap to assert. Aligns with Phase 2 signature work — the hash is what gets signed.

options.label: ID-set equality — downstream stages must reference the same AC ids, content can be regenerated
options.description: Loosest. Allows downstream to reformat for display.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Greenfield + brownfield ambiguity coverage in the admission path
questions.context: Both `greenfield-ambiguity-fixtures.test.ts` and `brownfield-ambiguity-fixtures.test.ts` exist in `packages/intent`. Phase 1 success criterion says 'every fixture in `examples/intents/`' is exercised through admission in CI.

options.label: Single parameterized admission test loops `examples/intents/**/*.json`, asserts expected verdict from manifest
options.description: Mirrors Q-06 a. One test, complete coverage. Adding a fixture = adding a manifest line.

options.label: Two parallel test files (greenfield, brownfield) with explicit fixture lists
options.description: Matches existing structure. Easier to read; harder to keep in sync with `examples/`.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Does Phase 1 introduce a GitHub Actions workflow, or stay pnpm-script-only?
questions.context: No `.github/workflows/` exists. Phase 10 (V1 Hardening + Dogfood) is where dogfood/CI gating is explicit. But the phase 1 success criterion includes 'in CI'.

options.label: Add a minimal `.github/workflows/verify.yml` running `pnpm install && pnpm run verify` on PR
options.description: Honors the literal 'in CI' criterion. Small surface, easy to expand in Phase 10.

options.label: No workflow yet — interpret 'in CI' as 'in the verify gate' and defer GH Actions to Phase 10
options.description: Minimizes Phase 1 scope. Risk: 'in CI' is technically unmet until Phase 10 ships.

options.label: Add the workflow but mark it advisory (no required-check) until Phase 10
options.description: Live but non-blocking. Lets contributors see CI signal without binding decisions.

options.label: Custom

questions.title: Is `ConfirmedIntent` admission signing in Phase 1 or strictly Phase 2 (GOV-06)?
questions.context: Phase 2 explicitly owns 'ConfirmedIntent carries an admission signature that downstream stages verify' (GOV-06). Q-04 option (b) and Q-10 option (b) both touch signing. Phase 1 could lay scaffolding without enforcement.

options.label: Strictly Phase 2 — Phase 1 uses brands/types, no signature material
options.description: Clean boundary. Phase 1 ships without crypto. Zero risk of Phase 2 having to undo work.

options.label: Phase 1 reserves a `signature: null` field on `ConfirmedIntent` for Phase 2 to fill
options.description: Schema slot reserved; downstream consumers can already pattern-match. No verification logic in Phase 1.

options.label: Phase 1 emits a content-hash (no key material) that Phase 2 upgrades to a signature
options.description: Hash is useful in its own right (replay detection, audit). Phase 2 swaps hash → signature.

options.label: Custom
